title: How to Make Moroccan Preserved Lemons with Sea Salt
description: Moroccan preserved lemons are incredibly easy to make using just two ingredients: lemons and sea salt. This traditional salt-curing method preserves the whole lemon while creating a flavorful ingredient with citrusy, floral, and umami notes from fermentation.

ingredients.item: g fresh lemons (about 4 to 5 small lemons)

steps: Wash all of the lemons and weigh out the salt in a bowl.
Cut the ends off the lemons, then starting at one end, cut each lemon into quarters without cutting all the way through.
Over a large bowl, fill the crevices of the lemons with generous amounts of salt until all the salt is used up.
Pack the whole lemons into a clean jar as tightly as possible, and lemon juice will be released in the process.
Once the jar is full, ensure all the lemons are submerged in lemon juice; if needed, juice an extra lemon and add the juice to the jar.
Place a lid on the jar and allow the lemons to cure at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Once opened, store in the refrigerator indefinitely, and rinse off any excess salt and brine before using the preserved lemons in recipes.
